No Poverty.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to poverty eradication.

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to hunger or food security.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to health or well-being.

Quality Education.reason: The course is intensely practical, providing vocational skills and knowledge for employment in media, directly aligning with Target 4.4.
evidence: intensely practical course aimed at providing participants with the skills and knowledge
be able to undertake most of the elements they will find
A. 媒介實作能力 Media Practice

Gender Equality.reason: While ICT skills are taught, the link to gender empowerment (Target 5.b) is extremely tenuous and not a stated course objective.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to water or sanitation.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to energy.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course explicitly prepares students for careers in the television newsroom, providing vocational training for decent jobs (Targets 8.3, 8.5).
evidence: have students confident as to what to expect should they step into a television newsroom
A. 媒介實作能力 Media Practice

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course focuses on advanced media production techniques, including 'virtual and electronic production' and 'video editing software', supporting technological upgrading and innovation (Target 9.5).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The ability to produce news programs could potentially promote social inclusion (Target 10.2), but this is an inferred application, not a direct course focus.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to sustainable cities or communities.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: News production skills can be used to raise public awareness for sustainable development (Target 12.8), though this is an indirect link.
evidence: Looking at social media and how to promote the now nearly finished news show

Climate Action.reason: News production skills can serve as a vehicle for awareness-raising on climate change (Target 13.3), but this is an indirect application.
evidence: production of news packages for both hard and soft news

Life Below Water.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to marine life or oceans.

Life on Land.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to terrestrial ecosystems or biodiversity.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The core output of producing a 'news programme' directly supports public access to information, a key aspect of Target 16.10.
evidence: students should have experiences of news writing

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The 'workshop by Forevertek' explicitly demonstrates a multi-stakeholder partnership for knowledge and technology sharing (Targets 17.6, 17.17).
evidence: There will be a workshop by Forevertek on virtual and electronic production techniques.
